title: eriogonum elatum subsp. typicum
intro: Eriogonum elatum subsp. typicum, also known as the mountain buckwheat or alpine buckwheat, is a perennial plant species within the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae) native to the western regions of North America. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Eriogonum elatum subsp. typicum include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate digestive issues, and support cardiovascular health, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health concerns. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be obtained by drying and processing the plant's leaves and flowers.
